items.title: Somalia: Somalia Says Turkish F-16 Airstrike Kills 35 Al-Shabab Fighters
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somalia's military, backed by Turkish forces, carried out airstrikes using F-16 fighter jets on Tuesday targeting al-Shabab positions in the Godey area of the Lower Shabelle region, the Ministry of Defence said.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somalia Says Turkish F-16 Airstrike Kills 35 Al-Shabab Fighters

items.title: Somalia: African Union, EU Discuss Support for Somalia Security Mission Amid Visa Tensions
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- The head of the African Union Support and Stabilization Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM) held talks on Tuesday with senior European Union officials on strengthening support for the mission and Somalia's security sector, amid diplomatic tensions over EU visa restrictions on Somali nationals.
items.titleIt: Somalia: African Union, EU Discuss Support for Somalia Security Mission Amid Visa Tensions

items.title: Egypt: Somalia Chairs Meeting of African Ambassadors in Cairo to Strengthen Continental Cooperation
items.description: [Shabelle] Cairo -- Somalia chaired the regular meeting of the Group of African Ambassadors in Cairo on Tuesday, with diplomats discussing ways to strengthen continental cooperation and deepen diplomatic coordination among African nations.
items.titleIt: Egypt: Somalia Chairs Meeting of African Ambassadors in Cairo to Strengthen Continental Cooperation

items.title: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu
items.description: The European Union is imposing new restrictions on visas for Somali nationals after a disagreement with Mogadishu over the return of irregular migrants to their home country.
items.titleIt: EU imposes visa restrictions on Somalis after readmission row with Mogadishu

items.title: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 30 GIU - The Ethiopian and Italian governments have signed a 70-million-euro financing package to support the macroeconomic reforms underway in Ethiopia and the country’s sustainable development agenda. The agreement was formally signed by Ethiopian Finance Minister Ahmed Shide and the Italian Ambassador to Ethiopia, Sem Fabrizi. The financing package, a testament to the strength and depth of the historic partnership between Ethiopia and Italy—founded on mutual trust and shared development priorities within the framework of the Mattei Plan for Africa— includes a €70 million c
items.titleIt: Italy-Ethiopia Agreement on Economic Reforms and Climate-Resilient Development

items.title: DRC opposition leader Sesanga banned from leaving the country
items.description: An opposition leader in the Democratic Republic of Congo said Monday he had been banned from leaving the country and stripped of his passport, condemning it as "intimidation" amid tensions with the government.
items.titleIt: DRC opposition leader Sesanga banned from leaving the country
